text.title: Cross-Platform Token Distribution Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying cross-platform-token-distribution; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: iOS team edits Tokens.swift directly to override a color; drift starts.
antipattern.root-cause: Pipeline missing or slow; iOS team works around it.
antipattern.fix: Pipeline is fast; CI fails on hand-edits; only canonical source is editable.

antipattern.symptom: Web uses `--color-primary` but iOS uses `accentColor`; refactor across platforms breaks.
antipattern.fix: Semantic names preserved across platforms; platform-native aliases live alongside, not instead.

antipattern.symptom: Dark mode tokens duplicated entirely instead of overriding semantic layer.
antipattern.fix: Themes override semantic layer only; primitives shared.
